overview: Performs word counts on the different parts of a set of documents following the [GMX-V 2.0 standard](http://www.xtm-intl.com/manuals/gmx-v/GMX-V-2.0.html). Word counts are saved as annotations on resources (text units, batches, documents, etc.) that can be consumed by downstream steps such as the Scoping Report Step.

countInBatch.help:
Adds a word count annotation aggregated across the entire batch. This annotation sums up counts from all batch items within the batch.

A word count annotation is **always** set on each individual text unit regardless of this setting — this controls the additional batch-level aggregation.

countInBatchItems.help:
Adds a word count annotation for each batch item. This aggregates the word counts of all documents within a single batch item.

A word count annotation is **always** set on each individual text unit regardless of this setting — this controls the additional batch-item-level aggregation.

countInDocuments.help:
Adds a word count annotation for each document. Useful when processing multiple documents and you need per-document totals.

A word count annotation is **always** set on each individual text unit regardless of this setting — this controls the additional document-level aggregation.

countInSubDocuments.help:
Adds a word count annotation for each sub-document. Sub-documents are logical sections within a document (e.g., embedded content or sub-flows).

A word count annotation is **always** set on each individual text unit regardless of this setting — this controls the additional sub-document-level aggregation.

countInGroups.help:
Adds a word count annotation for each group. Groups are structural containers that hold related text units together.

A word count annotation is **always** set on each individual text unit regardless of this setting — this controls the additional group-level aggregation.

bufferSize.help: Size of the internal text buffer used during word counting. A value of `0` means no explicit buffer size limit (uses the default).
bufferSize.values: `0` for default/unlimited, or a positive integer specifying the buffer size.

processingNotes: Word count annotations are **always** set on each individual text unit — the boolean options control whether *additional* aggregated annotations are set on higher-level resources (batches, documents, groups, etc.).
Word counts follow the [GMX-V 2.0 standard](http://www.xtm-intl.com/manuals/gmx-v/GMX-V-2.0.html) (Global information management Metrics eXchange — Volume).
The generated annotations can be consumed by downstream steps such as the **Scoping Report Step** to produce word count reports.

examples.title: Generate a scoping report with word counts
examples.description: A common pattern is to pair the Word Count step with the Scoping Report Step. The Word Count step annotates resources with GMX-V metrics, and then the Scoping Report Step reads those annotations to produce a summary report.
